parsed.answer: Formally/legally, no — U.S. laws are written to apply equally regardless of wealth. However, in practice, wealth creates significant disparities in legal outcomes: wealthy individuals can afford better legal representation, bail, and resources that affect case results. Critics argue the system functionally operates differently based on wealth even if the text of laws does not explicitly distinguish between rich and poor.
